Simulated Families

We simulated three different sizes of families. For each simulation run we subsetted the families to only include those families where the proband was a carrier of the PV. The simulation was done using the PedUtils package. The penetrance for non-carriers is based on the SEER penetrances in the PanelPRO Database. For carriers we specify a Weibull penetrance with the following parameters:

  • Alpha = 2.5

  • Beta = 50

  • Delta = 20

  • Gamma = 0.9

Family Sim 1- Uncensored

Censoring is based on a \[N(85,10)\]. The simulated age of death is centered at 85 with a standard deviation of 10, but values above ageMax (default = 94) are re-simulated to be centered around ageMax - 15.

Likelihood

The likelihood model in penet.fn is based on the Weibull distribution for carriers and, per default, the SEER peentrance data for non-carriers. For an individual \(i\), the model considers the following parameters:

  • \(\alpha\): Shape parameter of the Weibull distribution
  • \(\beta\): Scale parameter of the Weibull distribution
  • \(\delta\): Shift parameter for the age in the Weibull function
  • \(\gamma\): Asymptote parameter to adjust the scale of the Weibull function
  • \(\text{age}_i\): Age of the individual \(i\)
  • \(\text{aff}_i\): Affection status (1 if affected, 0 otherwise)

An indiviudal that develops cancer at \(\text{age}_i\) contributes a term \(f\) to the likelihood, which is just the density of the Weibull in our model. The contribution from an individual whose survival time is censored at is the survival probability \(S_i = 1 - F\) where \(F\) is corresponding cumulative distribution function of the Weibull.

The survival probability for individual \(i\) is calculated as: \[S_i = 1 - pweibull(\text{age}_i - \delta, \alpha, \beta) \times \gamma\] where pweibull is the CDF of the Weibull.

The density (for affected individuals) is calculated as:

\[ carrierLik_i = dweibull(\text{age}_i - \delta, \alpha, \beta) \times \gamma \]

For the function ( f(x; , , , ) ) we define:

\[ f(x; \alpha, \beta, \delta, \gamma) = \begin{cases} \gamma \left( \frac{\alpha}{\beta} \left( \frac{x - \delta}{\beta} \right)^{\alpha - 1} e^{-\left( \frac{x - \delta}{\beta} \right)^\alpha } \right), & x \geq \delta \\ 0, & x < \delta \end{cases} \]

And for the cumulative distribution function ( F(x; , , , ) ):

\[ F(x; \alpha, \beta, \delta, \gamma) = \begin{cases} \gamma \left( 1 - e^{-\left( \frac{x - \delta}{\beta} \right)^\alpha } \right), & x \geq \delta \\ 0, & x < \delta \end{cases} \]

The baseline risk \(\text{SEER\_baseline}_i\) is derived from SEER data:

\[ \text{noncarrierLik}_i = \begin{cases} \text{SEER\_baseline}_i & \text{if SeerNC is TRUE} \\ \text{Custom non-carrier penetrance model} & \text{otherwise} \end{cases} \]

For censored observations, the likelihood contribution for each individual is considered as the cumulative probability of being cancer-free, while for affected observations, it is based on the calculated density at their age of diagnosis.

Hence, the penetrance likelihood contribution for individual \(i\) and for all three potential genotypes 1/1 (non-carriers), 1/2 (heterozygous carrier), and 2/2 (homozygous carrier) is:

\[ \text{lik}_i = \begin{cases} 1 - \sum_{j=1}^{\text{age}_i} \text{SEER\_baseline}_{ij}, S_i, S_i & \text{if } \text{aff}_i = 0 \\ \text{nc.pen}_i, \text{c.pen}_i, \text{c.pen}_i & \text{if } \text{aff}_i = 1 \end{cases} \]

The adjustments for observed genotypes, gender-specific handling, and the condition for homozygotes are given as follows.

\begin{verbatim} if (data\(geno[i] == "1/1") penet.i[-1] <- 0 if (data\)geno[i] == “1/2”) penet.i[-2] <- 0 if (data$geno[i] == “2/2”) penet.i[-3] <- 0

if (!homozygote) { penet.i[3] <- 0 }

Based on the above a penetrance matrix is constructed for the pedigree. The (i, k)th component penet[i, k] of the penetrance matrix is the conditional probability of person i’s observed data \(x_i\), given that his or her actual genotype \(g_i\) is the kth genotype. Given a genetic model and a penetrance matrix, we then use clipp to calculate the loglikelihood for all pedigrees.

Based on the above model we then estimate two sets of sex-specific parameters of the Weibull distribution for males and females. The resulting estimated penetrance curves including credible intervals for a) a set of uncensored families and b) censored families is shown below.

Families Sim - Uncensored - Fixed Asymptote

Code 
dat<- simFamilies_C_1000_nocen_selected300

# Data Prep
for (i in seq_along(dat)) {
    if ("ID" %in% colnames(dat[[i]])) {
        colnames(dat[[i]])[colnames(dat[[i]]) == "PedigreeID"] <- "FamilyID"
    }
}

#  Data Prep
for (i in seq_along(dat)) {
    if ("ID" %in% colnames(dat[[i]])) {
        colnames(dat[[i]])[colnames(dat[[i]]) == "ID"] <- "SubjectID"
    }
}

for (i in seq_along(dat)) {
    # Add a new column "PedigreeID" with the list number
    dat[[i]]$PedigreeID <- i
}

# Exploring different priors 
prior_params_BRCA1 <- list(
    asymptote = list(g1 = 900, g2 = 100),
    threshold = list(min = 15, max = 30),
    median = list(m1 = 2, m2 = 2),
    first_quartile = list(q1 = 6, q2 = 3)
)

# Run Estimation procedure with default prior setting 
# Main Estimation for Female
out_sim_10k_simC300 <- PenEstim(
    data = dat,
    cancer_type = "Breast", gene_input = "BRCA1", n_chains = 4, n_iter_per_chain = 2000, 
    prior_params = prior_params_BRCA1, af = 0.9, burn_in = 0.1, sex = "Female", median_max = FALSE
)
Rejection rates:  0.841 0.8245 0.831 0.854
Code 
# print summary stats
out_sim_10k_simC300$summary_stats
  Median_Male  Median_Female   Threshold_Male Threshold_Female

Code 
plot_traceSingle(out_sim_10k_simC300$results[[1]])
Code 
plot_penetrance_sim(out_sim_10k_simC300$combined_chains, prob = 0.95, max_age = 94, sex = "Female",2.5,50,20,0.9)
plot(out_sim_10k_simC300$combined_chains$loglikelihood_current_results, type = "l", main = "Loglikelihood of Samples")
plot(out_sim_10k_simC300$combined_chains$loglikelihood_proposal_results, type = "l", main = "Loglikelihood of Proposals")

  # Autocorrelation plots
#acf(out_sim_10k_simC300$results[[1]]$first_quartile_male_samples, main = "ACF of First Quartile - Male")
#acf(out_sim_10k_simC300$results[[1]]$first_quartile_female_samples, main = "ACF of First Quartile - Female")

plot(out_sim_10k_simC300$combined_chains$median_male_results, out_sim_10k_simC300$combined_chains$first_quartile_male_results, xlim = c(1,100), ylim = c(1,100))
abline(a=0, b=1, col="red") # This draws a red line with slope 1 passing through the origin
Code 
plot(out_sim_10k_simC300$combined_chains$median_female_results, out_sim_10k_simC300$combined_chains$first_quartile_female_results, xlim = c(1,100), ylim = c(1,100))
abline(a=0, b=1, col="red") # This draws a red line with slope 1 passing through the origin

Families Sim3 - Censored - Fixed Asymptote

In this simulation the penetrance estimation is applied to the first set of families from sim1. The default prior settings are applied here with a uniform prior on the asymptote parameter. We estimate two sets of parameters of the Weibull distribution, seperately for male and female carriers. The respective non-carrier penetrance values are taken from the SEER baseline.

Code 
dat <- simfamilies_sim3

# Data Prep
for (i in seq_along(dat)) {
    if ("ID" %in% colnames(dat[[i]])) {
        colnames(dat[[i]])[colnames(dat[[i]]) == "PedigreeID"] <- "FamilyID"
    }
}

#  Data Prep
for (i in seq_along(dat)) {
    if ("ID" %in% colnames(dat[[i]])) {
        colnames(dat[[i]])[colnames(dat[[i]]) == "ID"] <- "SubjectID"
    }
}

for (i in seq_along(dat)) {
    # Add a new column "PedigreeID" with the list number
    dat[[i]]$PedigreeID <- i
}

# Exploring different priors 
prior_params_BRCA1 <- list(
    asymptote = list(g1 = 900, g2 = 100),
    threshold = list(min = 15, max = 40),
    median = list(m1 = 3, m2 = 2),
    first_quartile = list(q1 = 6, q2 = 3)
)

# Run Estimation procedure with default prior setting 
# Main Estimation for Female
out_sim_3 <- PenEstim(
    data = dat,
    cancer_type = "Breast", gene_input = "BRCA1", n_chains = 4, n_iter_per_chain = 5000, 
    prior_params = prior_params_BRCA1, af = 0.1, burn_in = 0.1, sex = "Female", median_max = FALSE
)
Warning in PenEstim(data = dat, cancer_type = "Breast", gene_input = "BRCA1", :
Low acceptance rate. Please consider running the chain longer.
Rejection rates:  0.9902 0.989 0.9868 0.9904
Code 
save(out_sim_3, file = "out_sim_3.Rdata")

# print summary stats
out_sim_3$summary_stats

# plot the scatterplot with the median and first quartile
plot(out_sim_3$combined_chains$median_male_results, out_sim_3$combined_chains$first_quartile_male_results, xlim = c(1,100), ylim = c(1,100))
abline(a=0, b=1, col="red") # This draws a red line with slope 1 passing through the origin
plot(out_sim_3$combined_chains$median_female_results, out_sim_3$combined_chains$first_quartile_female_results, xlim = c(1,100), ylim = c(1,100))
abline(a=0, b=1, col="red") # This draws a red line with slope 1 passing through the origin

Families Sim3 - Uncensored - Fixed Asymptote

In this simulation the penetrance estimation is applied to the first set of families from sim1. The default prior settings are applied here with a uniform prior on the asymptote parameter. We estimate two sets of parameters of the Weibull distribution, seperately for male and female carriers. The respective non-carrier penetrance values are taken from the SEER baseline.

Code 
dat <- simfamilies_sim3_UC

# Data Prep
for (i in seq_along(dat)) {
    if ("ID" %in% colnames(dat[[i]])) {
        colnames(dat[[i]])[colnames(dat[[i]]) == "PedigreeID"] <- "FamilyID"
    }
}

#  Data Prep
for (i in seq_along(dat)) {
    if ("ID" %in% colnames(dat[[i]])) {
        colnames(dat[[i]])[colnames(dat[[i]]) == "ID"] <- "SubjectID"
    }
}

for (i in seq_along(dat)) {
    # Add a new column "PedigreeID" with the list number
    dat[[i]]$PedigreeID <- i
}

# Exploring different priors 
prior_params_BRCA1 <- list(
    asymptote = list(g1 = 9, g2 = 100),
    threshold = list(min = 15, max = 30),
    median = list(m1 = 2, m2 = 2),
    first_quartile = list(q1 = 6, q2 = 3)
)

# Run Estimation procedure with default prior setting 
# Main Estimation for Female
out_sim_3 <- PenEstim(
    data = dat,
    cancer_type = "Breast", gene_input = "BRCA1", n_chains = 4, n_iter_per_chain = 5000, 
    prior_params = prior_params_BRCA1, af = 0.1, burn_in = 0, median_max = FALSE
)
Rejection rates:  0.8328 0.836 0.8344 0.8242
Code 
save(out_sim_3, file = "out_sim_3.Rdata")

# print summary stats
out_sim_3$summary_stats

 Mean   :950   Mean   :42.13   Mean   :0      Mean   :22.51   
 3rd Qu.:950   3rd Qu.:49.99   3rd Qu.:0      3rd Qu.:26.22   
 Max.   :950   Max.   :81.22   Max.   :0      Max.   :30.00   
 First_Quartile_Male First_Quartile_Female Asymptote_Male Asymptote_Female
 Min.   :800         Min.   :16.76         Min.   :1      Min.   :1

Code 
plot_traceSingle(out_sim_3$results[[1]])
Code 
plot_penetrance_sim(out_sim_3$combined_chains, prob = 0.95, max_age = 94,"Female",2.5,50,20,1)

# plot the scatterplot with the median and first quartile
plot(out_sim_3$combined_chains$median_male_results, out_sim_3$combined_chains$first_quartile_male_results, xlim = c(1,100), ylim = c(1,100))
abline(a=0, b=1, col="red") # This draws a red line with slope 1 passing through the origin
plot(out_sim_3$combined_chains$median_female_results, out_sim_3$combined_chains$first_quartile_female_results, xlim = c(1,100), ylim = c(1,100))
abline(a=0, b=1, col="red") # This draws a red line with slope 1 passing through the origin
